title: Ophrys ciliciana
taxonomy: Ophrys ciliciana is classified as a species of orchid in the family Orchidaceae. More specifically, it belongs to the genus Ophrys, which is a group of orchids commonly known as bee orchids. The genus Ophrys is further divided into several sections, and Ophrys ciliciana is part of the section Ophrys section Ophrys. Within the section, it is classified as Ophrys ciliciana, a distinct species endemic to the region of Cilicia in southern Turkey. This classification is based on the plant's unique characteristics, such as its flower shape and color.

intro: Ophrys ciliciana, also known as Cilician Bee Orchid, is a terrestrial orchid species native to the Mediterranean region, characterized by its unique, bee-like appearance. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in treating various conditions such as wounds, skin infections, and respiratory issues. Bioactive constituents of Ophrys ciliciana include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used in herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, often combined with other medicinal plants, to create remedies for various health conditions.
